headline: THE STRAIT IS SHUT. $113 OIL. MARKETS ON EDGE.
subheadline: The US-Iran war has choked the world's most critical energy artery — and every portfolio on Earth is feeling it.
hook: 27% of the world's oil flows through one narrow passage. Right now, almost nothing is getting through. Here's what that means for your money. 👇

bullets.title: Oil Above $113 & Climbing
bullets.detail: Brent crude hit $114/barrel after the US destroyed Iranian boats attacking commercial ships. The Strait of Hormuz is effectively closed — a ceasefire is fragile and near-zero shipping traffic continues.

bullets.title: Fed Frozen, Inflation Risk Rising
bullets.detail: The Fed is holding rates at 3.5–3.75% and won't cut. Prolonged oil above $100 threatens to reignite inflation — bad news for rate-sensitive stocks, bonds, and your mortgage.

bullets.title: Global Alliances Fracturing
bullets.detail: China & Russia vetoed a UN resolution to protect Hormuz shipping. France & UK announced a defensive escort mission. The Western alliance is splitting — and markets hate uncertainty.

watchOut: If the ceasefire collapses and oil spikes past $120, expect consumer spending to crater, emerging markets to bleed, and gold (already up 6% YTD) to surge further. Energy stocks are the only clear winner — for now.
